topic_intro.text: Object Oriented Programming (OOP) is a programming style where programs are built using objects and classes.

why_learn_this: Most real-world Java applications use OOP
OOP makes programs organized and reusable
Large applications become easier to manage
Games, apps, and websites use OOP
OOP is one of the most important Java concepts

explanations.text: OOP helps create programs using objects.
A class is like a blueprint of a car, and an object is the actual car created from that blueprint.
OOP helps programmers model real-world things like students, cars, users, and games.
OOP organizes software using classes, objects, methods, and properties.
Object Oriented Programming structures applications around interacting objects that encapsulate state and behavior.
Car is class and BMW is object.
All cars are created from one blueprint.
Factories create many cars from one design.
Objects are instances of class.
One class can generate multiple independent objects with different values.
Student is class while John is object.
Each student object stores different information.
Schools manage many student records.
Each object contains separate property values.
Objects allow independent state management within the same class structure.

class_creation.title: Creating a Class

explanations.text: A class creates a blueprint.
Classes define what data and actions objects will have.
A building plan is like a class blueprint.
Classes define properties and methods.
Classes provide reusable templates for object creation.

step_by_step: Class becomes ready for object creation
class_creation.real_life: Student blueprint for storing student data.

explanations.text: Objects are created from classes.
Objects are real usable versions of class blueprint.
A real car is created from car design blueprint.
Objects allocate memory for class members.
Objects encapsulate state and behavior defined by class.
Objects can store values.
Properties store information about object.
Apps save user details into objects.
Dot operator accesses object properties.
Object state changes through property assignment.
Object values can be printed.
Programs can display object information.
Apps display user profile details.
Dot operator retrieves property values.
Object state becomes accessible through property access.

methods_in_oop.title: Methods Inside Class

explanations.text: Methods define object actions.
Methods tell objects what they can do.
Cars can start, stop, and move just like objects use methods.
Methods define behavior inside classes.
Methods encapsulate reusable logic associated with objects.

methods_in_oop.real_life: Student can study, eat, and play.

explanations.text: Objects can call methods.
Methods run actions for objects.
Apps trigger actions using methods.
Method invocation executes behavior.
Objects interact with system functionality through method calls.

complete_program.title: Complete OOP Program

explanations.text: Complete programs combine classes, objects, and methods.
Objects store data and methods perform actions.
Real apps combine many objects together.
Classes encapsulate data and behavior.
OOP systems organize applications into reusable modular structures.

oop_benefits.title: Code Reusability
oop_benefits.explanation: Classes can be reused multiple times.

oop_benefits.title: Scalability
oop_benefits.explanation: Large applications become easier to manage.

wrong_vs_correct.why_wrong: Methods must be called using brackets.
Class names usually start with capital letter.

mini_project.title: Student Management System
mini_project.task: Create Student class with name, age, marks, and display method. Create multiple student objects and print details.
